meta.title: Per-step both-role figure charts (WDSF-first, researched)
meta.note: Real International Standard technique looked up per figure; WDSF preferred, dancecentral.info primary, cross-checked. NO fabrication: figures without a verifiable per-step source were removed from the catalog rather than guessed. Each entry records its source. Regenerate packages/domain/src/figure-charts.generated.ts with scripts/gen-figure-charts.mjs.

figures.corroboration: Back Feather = steps 4-6 of the Curved Feather to Back Feather page (slight R curve toward LOD)
figures.note: Turn amounts on the page (1/8) not given a clear L/R for the back feather; alignment shifts backing-DC to backing-LOD imply a slight right curve, omitted to avoid mis-stating direction.

figures.corroboration: dancecentral has no foxtrot Back Whisk page; mechanics identical to the Waltz Back Whisk (LF back CBMP > RF diag back > LF crosses behind in PP). Foxtrot whisk footwork (man HT/T/TH, lady TH/T/TH) per dance-forums/round-dancing.
figures.note: Charted with foxtrot SQQ timing and continuous rise; step count and footwork from the Back Whisk action (waltz 123 timing on source).

figures.corroboration: Steps 1-6 (the 7th LF-fwd step on the page is the start of the following figure).
Bounce Fallaway = steps 1-5 (bounce action rise/lower per count); Weave Ending = steps 6-8 (= weave steps 4-6, basic-weave page).
figures.note: 8 steps S&QQ QQQQ; the bounce action gives alternating rise/lower across the fallaway. The 9th step (LF fwd / RF back) on the page is the start of the following figure.
Authoritative source charts 3 weight-bearing steps (SSS); the stored 4th count corresponds to the LF/RF closing without weight on step 2, which is not modelled as a separate weight change.

figures.corroboration: Curved Feather = steps 1-3 of that page.

figures.note: 1/2 turn L occurs between steps 2-3, attributed to step 2.

figures.corroboration: https://longsteps.com.au/ballroom-blitz/2024/03/14/double-reverse-spin (foxtrot version: leader fewer steps, follower 4-step Reverse Heel Turn, SQ&Q)
figures.note: Leader dances 3 actions (step 3 = LF closes to RF on toe without weight). Follower dances SQ&Q (4 steps); the syncopated & (RF to side) is folded into the modelled 3 counts, with the final cross shown on count 3. dancecentral has no foxtrot DRS page; footwork from the (mechanically identical) Waltz DRS, corroborated for foxtrot by longsteps.
Lady's 5/8 pivot on step 3 capped at half_L per mapping rule.

figures.corroboration: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/slow-foxtrot/curved-feather-from-promenade-position (same SQQ feather action from PP, straight rather than curved)
figures.note: Straight Feather Ending from Promenade Position (RF fwd in CBMP/PP > LF fwd > RF fwd in CBMP OP). Technique values match the Curved Feather from PP minus the curve.

figures.corroboration: Feather Finish = steps 4-6 of the Reverse Turn.
Steps 1-3 (the 4th LF-fwd step on the page is the start of the following figure).
Heel Pull Finish = the closing 3 steps (4-6) of the Foxtrot Natural Turn; same data also on the Reverse Wave page
figures.note: No standalone page; this is the Natural Turn's heel-pull ending (man LF back, RF side heel-pull, LF fwd). Stored timing QSQ is wrong - the foxtrot heel-pull finish is danced SSS.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/waltz-hover-corte.html
figures.note: Foxtrot Hover Corte uses footwork identical to the Hover Corte sourced from the Waltz version; only the rhythm differs (Foxtrot SQQ vs Waltz 1-2-3). Requested start (Man RF back) and finish (transfer weight to RF, CP) match exactly. Closed Position throughout.
Charted steps 1-7 (SQQ QQQQ); the source's 8th step (LF fwd, S) is the feather-finish extension, excluded to match expectedCount 7 ending RF fwd in CBMP/OP.
Charted steps 1-3 (SQQ); the source's 4th step (RF fwd CBMP/OP, S) is the feather-finish extension, excluded to match expectedCount 3 ending LF diag fwd.
Charted steps 1-3 (SQQ); the source's 4th step (RF fwd in PP/CBMP, S) is the extension, excluded to match expectedCount 3 ending in Promenade.

figures.corroboration: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish page (steps 1-3 = the Closed Impetus proper)
figures.note: This is the Closed Impetus (man's heel turn, stays Closed Position, ends LF side/back). The foxtrot Closed Impetus is danced S Q Q, not the stored SSS; charted its first 3 steps.
This is the Open Impetus. Charted steps 1-3 (SQQ); the source's 4th step (RF fwd in PP/CBMP, S) is the extension, excluded to match expectedCount 3 ending in Promenade.

Charted steps 1-6 (SQQ SQQ); the source's 7th step (LF fwd, S) is the feather-finish extension, excluded to match expectedCount 6 ending RF fwd in CBMP/OP. Step 3/4 carry the heel-pull + hover (down-up-down rise).
Charted steps 1-5 (SQQQQ); the source's 6th step (LF fwd, S) is the feather-finish extension, excluded to match expectedCount 5 ending RF fwd in CBMP/OP.
Charted the first 3 steps (SQQ) of the full 6-step Natural Turn, ending RF back in Closed Position; lady does a heel turn on step 2.
Charted steps 1-7 (SQ&Q SQQ); the source's 8th step (LF fwd, S) is the feather-finish extension, excluded to match expectedCount 7 ending RF fwd in CBMP/OP. Step 4 is the twist (T of RF / H of LF).

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-natural-weave.html
figures.note: Steps 1-7 of the figure (the trailing S 'LF forward' in the source is the following figure's commencing step, not part of the weave).

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-natural-zig-zag-from-pp.html
figures.note: Steps 1-5 (trailing S 'LF forward' in the source is the following step, not part of the figure).

figures.source: http://idans.nl/workshop/standard/foxtrot/open_natural_turn.html (read via web.archive.org snapshot 20251115065207; live origin returning 522 at time of research)
figures.corroboration: Listed as a Silver figure in the idans/Ballroom Guide foxtrot syllabus.
figures.note: Silver Open Natural Turn (SQQ); step 1 in PP, steps 2-3 Closed. Source has no CBM column, so bodyActions omitted.

figures.corroboration: https://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-outside-swivel.html
figures.note: Single-step (S) swivel figure; man swivels on LF, lady on RF, finishing in PP. No rise.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-quick-open-reverse.html (both sources: 6 steps, SQ&Q + feather finish QQ)
figures.note: Authoritative sources give 6 steps (Quick Open Reverse SQ&Q + feather finish QQ), not the stored 7-count 'SQ&Q SQQ'; corrected to 6. Finish (RF fwd CBMP OP / weight on R) matches the stored finish; stored 'start RF fwd CBMP OP' does not match the figure's step 1 (LF fwd, closed) so it was disregarded.
Single-step pivot (up to 1/2 turn L); no rise.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-reverse-turn.html
figures.note: Steps 1-3 (first half of the Reverse Turn; finish LF back). Source lists the full turn with feather finish; only the SQQ first half is charted here.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/foxtrot-reverse-wave.html
figures.note: 9 steps. Source counts the last three as S S S; the stored 'QSQ' tail still totals 9 steps so the count matches and no correction is needed.

figures.corroboration: Closed Telemark; finish 'LF to side and slightly fwd, Closed Position' matches stored figure.
figures.note: Closed Telemark, steps 1-3 (the SQQ telemark; the source's step 4 RF fwd CBMP OP is a feather-ending and is not part of this 3-step figure).

figures.corroboration: Open Telemark portion (steps 1-3); finish in Promenade Position matches stored figure.
figures.note: Open Telemark, steps 1-3 (SQQ), finishing in PP; the feather-ending portion of the source figure is excluded.

figures.corroboration: https://beautifulballroomdancing.com/foxtrot-three-step/
figures.note: dancecentral table lists a 4th row (RF fwd, CBM) which is the commencing step of the following figure; charted only the 3 Three Step steps (LF-RF-LF, SQQ).
dancecentral lists a 5th row (LF fwd, S) which is the commencing step of the next figure; charted the 4 Top Spin steps (QQQQ). Top Spin turns L throughout.
dancecentral lists an 8th row (LF fwd, S) which is the commencing step of the next figure; charted the 7 Weave steps (SQQ QQQQ). Turns attributed to the earlier step of each 'between X-Y' pair.

figures.corroboration: ISTD/IDTA foxtrot syllabus: the Foxtrot Whisk is danced the same as the International Style Waltz Whisk (timed SQQ in foxtrot). See http://idans.nl/workshop/standard/foxtrot.html
figures.note: No dedicated foxtrot Whisk page on dancecentral; foxtrot Whisk is identical in technique to the Waltz Whisk per syllabus. Follower 1/4 R turn (btwn 1-2) attributed to step 1.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-back-whisk.html (confirms QS figure, SSS, up to 1/4 R; no per-step table there)
figures.note: Back Whisk was adapted from the Waltz to the Quickstep; per-step technique taken from the identical Waltz Back Whisk, danced SSS in QS. Leader=man's back-whisk part.

figures.corroboration: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/quickstep/forward-lock (partner part)
figures.note: Leader dances Back Lock, follower dances Forward Lock. Zero turn. Cross-in-front steps (backing) encoded as direction 'back' since the enum only has 'behind' for crosses.
Charted the 3-step Cross Chasse proper (LF fwd, side, close); the page's surrounding RF-fwd steps are the preceding/following figure.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-cross-swivel.html (Gold figure)
figures.note: Danced SS; the '&' cross/swivel action between the two slows is folded into the 1/4 L turn on step 1. Leader finishes RF fwd in CBMP, Outside Partner.
Lady dances 4 weight changes over the man's 3 counts; her steps 3+4 (RF side, then LF cross in front) are folded into count 3 (footwork T/TH, direction side).
Charted the 6-step Fishtail (SQQQQS); the page's 7th step (RF fwd in CBMP) is the lead into the following figure. Follower cross-in-front steps (backing) encoded as direction 'back'.

figures.corroboration: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/quickstep/back-lock (partner part)
figures.note: Leader dances Forward Lock, follower dances Back Lock. Zero turn. Follower cross-in-front (backing) encoded as direction 'back'.
Charted the 6-step Four Quick Run (SQQQQS); the page's 7th step (RF fwd in CBMP) is the lead into the following figure. Follower cross-in-front (backing) encoded as direction 'back'.
Charted the 3-step Hover Corte (RF back, LF side/hover, transfer back to RF); the page's 4th step (LF back in CBMP) is the lead-out. Step-3 transfer (side/slightly back) encoded as direction 'back'.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-closed-impetus.html (confirms SSS, 3 steps)
figures.note: Closed Impetus. Man dances the heel turn (LF closes to RF). dancecentral table listed a 4th step which is the following figure's lead-in; the figure proper is 3 steps SSS.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-open-impetus.html (confirms QS Open Impetus, SSS)
figures.note: Open Impetus to Promenade Position. Per-step technique from the identical Open Impetus (adapted to QS SSS timing); the source's 4th step (fwd in PP/CBMP) is the lead-out. Man does the heel turn, finishes LF diag fwd in PP.

figures.corroboration: Pivot action = step 4 of the Natural Pivot Turn (LF back, ball-of-foot pivot up to 1/2 R, RF held in CBMP)
figures.note: Single pivot step (timing S). Charted from the pivot step of the Natural Pivot Turn, matching the batch start/finish (LF back, weight on LF, RF held).

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-natural-spin-turn.html
figures.note: Step 4 is the spin pivot (LF back, up to 1/2 R, no rise). Pivot footwork recorded as TH/HT.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-natural-turn.html
figures.note: Batch wants the 3-step half (SQQ, finish RF closes to LF); charted steps 1-3 of the dancecentral full Natural Turn.

figures.corroboration: Batch wants the 3-step Open Reverse Turn (SQQ, finish LF back in CBMP/OP) = steps 1-3 of the Quick Open Reverse (the 4th step RF back is the quick-open extension).
figures.note: Charted steps 1-3 of the Quick Open Reverse to match expectedCount 3 and finish 'LF bwd in CBMP'.

figures.corroboration: dancecentral 'Progressive Chasse' is the to-Left figure (Newcomer); charted steps 1-4 (SQQS) to match batch finish 'LF to side and slightly fwd'. 5th step (RF fwd CBMP) is the link to next figure.
figures.note: Charted steps 1-4 of the 5-step dancecentral entry to match expectedCount 4.

figures.corroboration: Charted steps 1-4 (SQQS) to match batch finish 'RF to side and slightly Bwd'. dancecentral lists a 5th step (LF back CBMP) as the link to outside-partner.
figures.note: Charted steps 1-4 of the 5-step dancecentral entry to match expectedCount 4.

figures.corroboration: Man footwork THT stated by dancecentral; lady forward-pivot footwork (HT) per pivot technique (dancecentral natural-pivot-turn gives HTH for the forward pivot).
figures.note: Single pivot step (S). Man RF back in CBMP, body turns strongly L, up to 1/2 turn L.

figures.corroboration: Stored Reverse Turn (SQQ, LF fwd -> LF closes to RF) matches dancecentral Quickstep Chasse Reverse Turn exactly
figures.note: The Quickstep 'Reverse Turn' is the Chasse Reverse Turn (SQQ, 3 steps); start/finish/timing match.
Follower step 2 'LF crosses in front of RF' mapped to direction forward (no cross-in-front token).

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/quickstep-running-cross-chasse.html (confirms figure + SQQS timing); https://www.ballroomgenie.com/figures/quickstep/running-cross-chasse-to-promenade-position/crossley-marriner/
figures.note: Charted from dancecentral.info's combined Cross Chasse / Running Cross Chasse table; mapped steps 1-4 to the requested 4-step SQQS figure (RF fwd CBMP/OP through LF fwd L-side-leading to Closed). The source's step 5 (RF fwd CBMP/OP) is the entry into the following figure and is excluded. Runs straight down LOD, no turn; 'S' (straight) sway treated as none.
Charted steps 1-3 (SQQ) per stored timing; the source's 4th step (RF fwd CBMP OP / LF back CBMP) is the lead-out into the next figure. Finish 'LF fwd L side leading' = leader step 3.

figures.corroboration: Natural Spin Turn pivot footwork (THT leader / HT follower) corroborated at dancecentral natural-spin-turn
figures.note: Running Natural Turn = Running Right Turn (synonymous). Stored 10-step timing SQQ SSSS QQS exactly matches the source's steps 1-10; the source's 11th step (RF fwd CBMP OP) is the lead-out. Sections: 1-4 Natural Pivot, 5-7 Half Natural Turn (Lady heel turn), 8-10 Running Finish.

figures.corroboration: Pivot footwork corroborated at dancecentral natural-spin-turn
figures.note: dancecentral consistently gives the Running Spin Turn as 5 steps SSQQS (Spin Turn portion steps 1-2 + Running portion steps 3-5), not the stored 7-step 'SQQ SQQS'. Charted the verified 5-step figure. The stored start 'RF fwd' / finish 'RF bwd R side leading' do not match the canonical figure (leader begins LF back); they likely describe the follower / an interior step.

Charted the 6 quicks (QQQQQQ) per stored timing; the source's 7th step (RF fwd CBMP OP, S) is the lead-out. Finish 'LF fwd slightly leftward' = leader step 6 (LF diag fwd). Follower step 5 'LF crosses in front of RF' mapped to direction forward.

Quickstep 'Telemark' = Closed Telemark steps 1-3 (SQQ) per stored timing; the source's 4th step (RF fwd CBMP OP) is the lead-out. Follower does a heel turn on steps 1-2. Finish 'LF to side and slightly fwd' = leader step 3.

figures.corroboration: Open Telemark = Closed Telemark steps 1-3 ending in Promenade Position (the only difference); per dancecentral search note Quickstep Closed Telemark footwork = Waltz Closed Telemark
figures.note: No standalone per-step Open Telemark page exists for quickstep (open-telemark 404s). Charted from the verified Closed Telemark (identical footwork HT/T/TH for both roles on steps 1-3); the defining difference is that step 3 opens to Promenade Position (Lady turns to PP rather than completing the closed-position turn).
Charted the 'Along Side of Room' variant steps 1-4 (SQQS) per stored timing; the source's steps 5-8 are the running finish lead-out. Finish 'RF to side and slightly fwd' = leader step 4.
Charted the 'At Corner' variant steps 1-4 (SQQS) per stored timing; the source's steps 5-8 are the running finish lead-out. Finish 'RF to side and slightly fwd' = leader step 4.
Q&Q, 3 steps (step 2 on the '&'). Step 2 'move foot halfway toward the other' mapped to direction close. Sway mirrors the Tipsy to Right pattern from the same source.
Q&Q, 3 steps (step 2 on the '&'). Step 2 'move foot halfway toward the other' mapped to direction close.

figures.corroboration: dancecentral lists V6 steps 1-7 as the figure proper (step 8 begins the following Forward Lock); finish 'LF to side and slightly fwd' = leader step 7
figures.note: dancecentral times the 7 steps S Q Q S S Q Q (stored had SQQS QQS); count unchanged at 7. Backward-lock entry (steps 1-4) then prep/turn to CP.

figures.corroboration: Standard Weave from PP — body technique is dance-independent; dancecentral has no separate quickstep page. idans.nl confirms the weave-from-PP family is danced in quickstep. Charted to stored quickstep timing SQQ SQQ (waltz figure's steps 1-6; finish 'LF to side & slightly fwd CP' = leader step 6).
figures.note: Steps 1-6 of the standard weave; the waltz table's 7th step (RF fwd OP) is the next figure's lead-in and is excluded to match the stored 6-count finish in Closed Position.

figures.corroboration: Standard Whisk — identical body technique across Standard dances; charted to stored quickstep timing SSS (3 steps), finishing LF crossed behind in Promenade Position.
figures.note: Quickstep dances the whisk flat/with reduced rise vs waltz; rise pattern (commence/continue/lower) retained from the shared technique.

figures.corroboration: Standard Wing — body technique is dance-independent. This variant enters from Outside Partner (preceding figure ends OP); leader holds weight on RF while the lady curves from his right to his left side. Charted to stored quickstep timing S(QQ).
figures.note: Leader keeps weight on RF throughout — steps 2-3 are the LF closing without weight to Wing Position; turn/sway/footwork per the shared Wing technique. Entry on OP (CBMP) rather than PP distinguishes this from 'Wing from Promenade Position'.

figures.corroboration: Standard Wing entered from Promenade Position (the wing portion of Open Telemark and Wing). Body technique dance-independent; charted to stored quickstep timing S(QQ).
figures.note: Same wing action as 'wing' but step 1 enters in Promenade Position (RF/LF fwd and across in PP & CBMP). Leader holds weight on RF; steps 2-3 are LF closing without weight to Wing Position.

figures.corroboration: First 6 steps (Zig-Zag + Back Lock) of dancecentral's combined Zig-Zag/Back-Lock/Running-Finish; finish 'RF diag bwd CP' = leader step 6, before the Running Finish.
figures.note: Steps 1-2 are the Zig-Zag (two slow turning steps); steps 3-6 are the Back Lock (SQQS). Sway straight throughout per source; rise begins at step 3 (no foot rise).
Tango: no rise. Turn 1/4 L falls between steps 2-3, attributed to step 2.
Tango: no rise. Man 1/4 R between steps 2-3 (on step 2) then 1/8 R on step 3; lady turn not specified per-step in source so omitted.
Tango: no rise. Man step 3 is LF crossing in front of RF (verified verbatim); mapped to direction 'forward' as the closest token (no cross-in-front token exists).

Tango: no rise. Counts QQ&S. Step 3 is a no-weight brush (foot slightly off floor) - footwork given as 'T' (ball/toe, the part nearest the floor in a brush). Steps 3 and 4 carry no weight. May be danced without the 1/8 turn.
Tango: no rise. Counts S Q Q Q Q S. Man rotates R through steps 3-6 but source gives no per-step turn fractions, so turn omitted. Step 6 footwork 'BH and IE of B' simplified to BH.

figures.corroboration: https://www.dancecentral.info/ballroom/international-style/tango/the-chase (Method 4 = Chase followed by Progressive Link)
figures.note: Tango: no rise. This 2-step (QQ) ending IS the Progressive Link (matches stored start LF fwd CBMP / finish RF to side & slightly back in PP); charted from the Progressive Link technique page.
Tango: no rise. Counts SQQS. Lady makes 1/4 L on step 3.
Tango: no rise. Counts QQQQ. Turn 1/8 between steps 1-2 (man L, lady R) attributed to step 1. Fallaway position on steps 2-3 omitted (not in position enum).

Tango: no rise. Stored 'Fallaway IN Promenade' = the Fallaway Promenade (SQQ SQQ, matches stored start/finish). 1/4 R early (between 1-4) on step 1; 1/8 L on step 4 (man) / step 5 (lady). Fallaway position on steps 4-5 omitted.
Stored timing QQS (3) corrected to QQQQ (4 steps) per dancecentral. Turn 3/4 L on the slip pivot capped to half_L. Stored finish names PP; dancecentral ends the slip pivot in CBMP facing center.
1/4 L between 1-2. No rise & fall (tango).
1/8 L between 1-2; Lady 1/4 R between 3-4.
1/4 L between 1-2. Syncopated QQ&S; step 3 is the very quick closing action.

figures.corroboration: https://www.dance-forums.com/threads/mini-5-step.31217/ (Mini 5 Step = Five Step danced with no turn or up to 1/4 L)
figures.note: No dedicated per-step page. The Mini Five Step is the Five Step danced with reduced/no turn (<=1/4 L), often into a corner. Per-step footwork/direction taken from the Five Step (dancecentral); the step-1 turn is omitted to reflect the reduced-turn 'mini' definition.

Stored as 'Natural Rock Turn'; charted from dancecentral Rock Turn (SQQSQQS, 7 steps) - exact start (RF fwd, Closed) and finish (RF closes slightly back, Closed) match. 1/4 R steps 1-3, 1/4 L steps 4-6. Step 3 / step 4 are the rock (replace).

'Natural Turn from PP' charted from dancecentral Natural Promenade Turn (SQQS, PP start). Total 3/4 R, attributed (capped to half_R) to step 3. Stored finish describes an under-turned PP ending; dancecentral's standard finish (Man RF fwd CBMP, DW new LOD) is shown.

Charted from dancecentral 'Natural Twist Turn' page, which documents the 6-step from-PP figure (S Q Q S Q Q, start LF side in PP). Man twists R (capped to half_R on step 5); per-step Lady turn not broken out by source (total = full turn R).
Lady turns 1/4 L between 2-3 to back-DW; ends in Outside Partner (CBMP).
Charted as the Lady Outside version (QQSQQS, 6 steps), matching stored closed finish. 3/8 L between 1-2 and 5-6.

figures.corroboration: ISTD outside swivel (Method 1 - Man does not change direction)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. Whole-foot/foot-flat steps noted as WF.

figures.corroboration: ISTD outside swivel (Method 2 - Man swivels left)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Reverse Outside Swivel (Silver)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. QQSQQ = 5 steps.

figures.corroboration: ballroomindex.weebly.com/tango-progressive-link.html
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Progressive Side Step (Newcomer)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn (total turn 3/4 L)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. QQSSQQSQQS = 10 steps.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Promenade Link turned to L (Reverse Promenade Link): Man 1/8 L 1-2, Lady 3/8 L 2-3
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. Footwork from base Promenade Link; turn amounts per turned-to-L variation.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Promenade Link turned to R: Man 1/8 R on 3 to face Wall (Closed), Lady 1/8 L on 3
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. Footwork from base Promenade Link; turn amounts per turned-to-R variation.

figures.corroboration: Charted from the QQ&QQS Tango reverse turn (Basic Reverse Turn / Viennese Cross); identical timing, start (LF fwd) and finish (RF closes to LF slightly back)
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. No dedicated Quick Reverse Turn page; QQ&QQS reverse turn matches the stored start/finish exactly.

figures.corroboration: ballroomindex.weebly.com/tango-reverse-pivot.html (rotation between 1/4 and 1/2 to L). Reverse Pivot is mechanically identical across Standard dances; tango has no rise.
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. Single & step; turn capped at half_L (source: up to 1/2 L).

figures.corroboration: ISTD LF Rock (QQS), L side leading
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted.

figures.corroboration: ISTD RF Rock (QQS) in CBMP, L shoulder leading
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted.

figures.corroboration: ISTD Tango Walk (Newcomer); two forward walks (Man LF, RF), no rise, slight CBM, in CBMP
figures.note: Tango: rise omitted. Forward walks heel-led (no rise); Lady back walks toe-heel.

figures.corroboration: Mirror of RF Forward Change (Natural to Reverse); footwork HT/T/TH (fwd) and TH/T/TH (bwd) is the standard ISTD change-step footwork.
figures.note: Leader dances the LF Backward Change (the Lady's part of the Natural-to-Reverse change); follower dances the RF Forward Change. Change step turns ~1/4 total to the right to set up the next Reverse Turn.

figures.corroboration: Footwork HT/T/TH (fwd) and TH/T/TH (bwd) per standard ISTD change step; confirmed on a second focused fetch of the same page.
figures.note: Leader dances the LF Forward Change; follower dances the RF Backward Change. Change turns ~1/4 total to the left to set up the next Natural Turn.

figures.corroboration: Total rotation ~one full turn over the two bars; shared rise & fall commence/up/lower per bar matches standard ISTD/WDSF technique.
figures.note: No foot crossing in VW Natural Turn; feet close on 3 and 6. Shared rise taken from the leader column.

figures.corroboration: Mirror of the Natural Turn turning left; ~one full turn over two bars, shared rise & fall per standard ISTD/WDSF.
figures.note: Source labels the count-3/6 closing step 'crosses in front'; mapped to 'close' (no front-cross token, and VW closes the feet). Shared rise from leader column.

figures.corroboration: Mirror of LF Forward Change (Reverse to Natural). A change step is 3 counts (123); the stored 20-count timing string was corrupt.
figures.note: Stored expectedCount was 20 from a corrupted timing string ('QQQQ QQQQ QQQQ QQQQ Q(Q Q)aQ'); a VW change step is one bar / 3 steps, corrected to '123'. Leader dances the RF Backward Change; follower dances the LF Forward Change.

figures.corroboration: Footwork HT/T/TH (fwd) and TH/T/TH (bwd) per standard ISTD change step.
figures.note: Leader dances the RF Forward Change; follower dances the LF Backward Change. Change turns ~1/4 total to the right to set up the next Reverse Turn.

figures.corroboration: dancecentral lists this as 'Back Lock'; matches batch start (LF back in CBMP) and finish (RF diag back).
figures.note: Leader's '&' step (LF crosses in front of RF, a backward lock) modelled as 'diagonal' — no front-cross token exists; follower's '&' is a true cross-behind.

Source charts 7 steps; this figure ends at step 6 (LF to side & slightly fwd, matching batch finish). The 7th step (RF fwd OP) is the lead into the next figure and is excluded, giving the stored 6-count '123 123'.
4 steps over timing 12&3 (counts 1, 2, &, 3).
LF Closed Change (Reverse-to-Natural): LF fwd, RF side, LF closes.
RF Closed Change (Natural-to-Reverse): RF fwd, LF side, RF closes.

figures.corroboration: No standalone 'cross-hesitation-from-pp' page; charted from the Cross Hesitation portion of dancecentral's Open Impetus and Cross Hesitation, counts relabelled 1-3.
figures.note: Leader steps once (RF fwd in PP) then closes LF without weight and holds (timing 1,2,3 with weight remaining on RF); Lady dances a full chasse-like 3 steps.

Leader dances 3 steps (123). Lady's syncopated 12&3 is mapped to 3 model-steps: her '&' (RF to side) is folded into count 3, charted as her final crossing step (LF crosses in front, modelled as 'forward').

Stored timing '1&23' does not match the figure; dancecentral documents '1 2 3 &' (1,1,1/2,1/2) — slip pivot taken on the final '&'. Steps ordered LF fwd / RF back fallaway / LF back CBMP fallaway / RF back slip pivot. Fallaway position has no enum token, so it is omitted (CBMP marked where stated).

figures.corroboration: longsteps.com.au / ballroomdancers.com: hesitation half is body rise only, no foot rise; no rise in the heel pull (stay low).
figures.note: Charted from the hesitation half (steps 4-6) of dancecentral's Hesitation Change page, matching the batch start (LF back) and finish (LF closes w/o weight, weight on RF). Rise: primary chart left rise blank for these counts; set per corroboration (NFR on the heel pull, body rise as feet close).
Count 3 is the corte (weight transfer back onto RF for man).
Closed Impetus. Man's count 2 is a heel turn (footwork H). Late rise (end of 2).

figures.corroboration: Closed Impetus page corroborates man's count-1 back-step footwork (TH).
figures.note: Open Impetus (first 3 steps of the Open Impetus & Wing page); ends in PP. Man's count 2 is a heel turn (H). Source text rendered man count-1 footwork as 'Heel'; corrected to TH (a back step) per the Closed Impetus chart of the identical opening step.

figures.corroboration: http://ballroomindex.weebly.com/waltz-natural-spin-turn.html (rotation: steps 1-3 = 3/8 R, steps 4-6 = 5/8 to 7/8 R)
figures.note: Counts 4-5 are the pivot in CBMP; man's count 4 footwork is toe-heel-toe (T/H/T) through the pivot.

Charted as the Turning Lock to R (= the natural/right-turning lock that ends in PP); its start (RF back, R side leading) and finish (LF diag fwd in PP) match this figure exactly. Count & is the lock (man crosses LF in front, lady crosses RF behind). Sway omitted: source gives a variable sway option.
Starts in Outside Partner (CBMP), finishes in Closed Position.
Danced in CBMP/Outside Partner; early, sharp rise (axis on lady's RF). Man's count 1 footwork is a toe pivot (T/H/T). Lady's count-1 turn (5/8) capped at half_R.

figures.corroboration: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chass%C3%A9_(waltz)
figures.note: Reverse (left-turning) figure that travels to the leader's right; count & is the chasse close.

figures.corroboration: Waltz page 404s; charted from the Quickstep Quick Open Reverse (identical footwork/turn family). Rhythm S Q Q S maps to the stored 1&23.
figures.note: Reverse turn, lady steps outside (count 2 / our step 3 in CBMP).

figures.corroboration: Standard ISTD pivot footwork (back foot Toe-Heel, forward foot Heel-Toe); dancecentral describes toe-heel/heel-toe pivot action, up to 1/2 turn L in CBMP
figures.note: Single-step figure on '&'. Pivot danced low (no rise). Up to 1/2 turn to L; body turns strongly to L giving CBMP.
Closed Position throughout; turns left. CBM on the forward/back driving steps (1 and 4).

figures.corroboration: dancecentral 'Turning Lock' (turns L); first 4 steps (1&23) match stored start/finish/timing
figures.note: Charted the 4-step core (counts 1 & 2 3) of the Turning Lock; the source's 5th step (RF fwd in CBMP, Outside Partner) is the link into the following figure and is omitted to match the stored 4-count finish (LF to side and slightly fwd).

figures.corroboration: DanceSportVerband C-class Standard extension syllabus lists Running Cross Chasse as a Waltz figure (start RF fwd CBMP OP); dancesport.org.au/syllabus/standard/waltz/running-cross-chasse.php (video)
figures.note: DanceNote Slow Waltz chart, 4 steps timing 1&23. Follower step 1 carries NFR (no foot rise); shared rise rendered as 'commence'.

figures.corroboration: DanceSportVerband C-class Standard extension syllabus references Running Finish as a Waltz figure; dancecentral lists it under Quickstep, but the Waltz version is real
figures.note: DanceNote Slow Waltz chart, 3 steps timing 123. Man backs in CBMP OP; turn 3/8 R (man, body less) / 1/4 R (lady) occurs 1-2.

figures.corroboration: Steps 1-3 from DanceNote Slow Waltz Natural Spin Turn (https://dancenote.net/mixfigure_one.php?figure_name=w_naturalSpinTurn&lang=en); start/finish/7-step timing corroborated by DanceSportVerband syllabus and ballroomindex.weebly.com/waltz-running-spin-turn.html
figures.note: Composite of two DanceNote Slow Waltz charts: steps 1-3 = Natural Spin Turn opening (123); steps 4-7 = DanceNote 'Running Spin Turn' running ending (1&23). The running chart's step 1 (LF back, 1/2 pivot R to LOD) is identical to the Natural Spin Turn's pivot, so the join is exact.

Closed Telemark. Lady dances a heel turn on count 2. Charted 3 steps (1 2 3); the source's 4th step (RF fwd in CBMP, Outside Partner) is the link into the next figure and is omitted to match the stored closed-position finish.

figures.corroboration: Open Telemark portion (steps 1-3) of the Open Telemark and Wing; ends in Promenade Position
figures.note: Open Telemark (Telemark to PP). Lady dances a heel turn on count 2; figure finishes in Promenade Position.

Charted 6 steps (123 123) to match the stored finish (LF to side and slightly fwd). The source describes a 7-step Weave from PP; its 7th step (RF fwd in CBMP, Outside Partner) is the link into the following figure and is omitted.
Basic Whisk ending in Promenade Position (Lady turns 1/4 R, Man no turn). Crossing step (count 3) crosses behind into PP.

figures.corroboration: Wing portion (steps 4-6) of the Open Telemark and Wing
figures.note: Wing: Man steps RF fwd in CBMP then closes LF without weight over counts 2-3 while the Lady walks around to his left, finishing in Wing Position. Leader uses no foot rise through the figure. Entered from Outside Partner / CBMP (vs the PP entry of Wing from PP).

figures.corroboration: Wing portion (steps 4-6) of the Open Telemark and Wing, entered from Promenade Position
figures.note: Wing entered from Promenade Position (RF fwd and across in CBMP/PP). Man closes LF without weight over counts 2-3 while the Lady walks around to his left into Wing Position. Leader uses no foot rise.
